narrative:
Sunshine will give way to a few extra clouds by this afternoon with high temperatures still running around 10-degrees cooler than normal. Under clear skies tonight, frost and/or freeze conditions are likely in the valleys by sunup. After tomorrow morning, the chill gets replaced by another surge of warmth, propelling us back into above-average territory for the second half of the workweek. Dry weather is set to continue, until shower potential returns over the weekend.

This week's Monday Message addresses the temperature roller-coaster, drought status, and improving rain prospects for next week.

The RaysWeather.Com Photo Contest is underway for photos to appear in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar. We are open for submissions through April 19. We broke all records with 2026 calendar sales and look to top that with the 2027 calendar — it's our 24th calendar. Come on! Hit us with your best shot!

Chillin’ To Grillin’

Last Updated Mon April 20 at 10:25AM by Scott Stephens

Sunshine will give way to a few extra clouds by this afternoon with high temperatures still running around 10-degrees cooler than normal. Under clear skies tonight, frost and/or freeze conditions are likely in the valleys by sunup. After tomorrow morning, the chill gets replaced by another surge of warmth, propelling us back into above-average territory for the second half of the workweek. Dry weather is set to continue, until shower potential returns over the weekend.

This week's Monday Message addresses the temperature roller-coaster, drought status, and improving rain prospects for next week.

Forecast Discussion

A weak/dry cold front will reinforce the cool air in place today. It comes through with little fanfare other than a few extra clouds. Under clear skies tonight and with building high pressure, expect a frost or freeze by daybreak.

Tuesday will be sunny and milder by afternoon. The warming trend will continue through Friday with warming temperatures each successive day. It should be outstanding weather to get outside and enjoy if you are able.

Some changes commence over the weekend as a frontal system approaches by next Sunday. This will bring our next opportunity for sorely-needed rainfall by Saturday afternoon and Sunday. Rainfall amounts do not look particularly significant at this juncture, but we are hopeful that some alteration in the pattern might set us up for some better rainfall potential down the road.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

This week's roller-coaster temperature ride continues with more dry conditions perpetuating the drought. As we look further ahead, there may be better news on sending western moisture our way for week two.
